topics.title: Deep Tech
topics.executive_summary: Recent breakthroughs span quantum sensing, physics-native computing, and brain restoration: CERN detected hints of physics beyond the Standard Model, UC Davis shrunk lab-quality spectrometry to a grain-of-sand chip, and Texas A&M's nasal spray reversed cognitive aging in early tests.

stories.headline: CERN detects strongest hints yet of physics beyond Standard Model
stories.body: <cite index="7-8">Scientists working at CERN's Large Hadron Collider may be seeing the strongest hints yet of physics beyond the Standard Model—the decades-old theory that explains the fundamental particles.</cite>

stories.headline: UC Davis shrinks lab spectrometer to grain-of-sand AI chip
stories.body: <cite index="7-8">An AI-powered chip from UC Davis can analyze light and chemicals using a device tiny enough to fit almost anywhere, combining smart silicon sensors with machine learning.</cite>

stories.headline: Nasal spray reverses brain aging by restoring cellular energy
stories.body: <cite index="7-7">Researchers at Texas A&M developed a nasal spray that appears to reverse brain aging by calming inflammation and restoring the brain's energy systems, with memory and cognitive function improved for months after just two doses.</cite>

stories.headline: Quantum sensor detects energy below one zeptojoule
stories.body: <cite index="5-2,5-3">Researchers built an ultra-sensitive sensor capable of detecting unimaginably small amounts of energy below one zeptojoule, relying on fragile superconducting materials that react to even the slightest temperature change.</cite>

stories.headline: Penn creates hybrid light-matter particle for ultra-efficient AI
stories.body: <cite index="5-10">Researchers at Penn created a hybrid light-matter particle that could dramatically speed up AI computing while using far less energy, potentially replacing electronic computing with light-based processes.</cite>
